rationale: Bolton (2003) is used as more than a passing citation: the citing paper summarizes it in Table 1 as a key prior finding on scenario-based optimism in new product evaluation. It is also cited in the core framing that some judgment biases in NPD are resistant to correction and can even intensify with more effort. These citations help motivate the paper's hypotheses and interpretation of its own customer-imagery bias results.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Bolton (2003) as an empirical and conceptual foundation showing that nonanalytic, imagery-like processing can produce optimistic bias in new product judgments and resist debiasing. It then builds its own argument around a related mechanism (customer mental imagery during screening).
